```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  k <- sample(seq(1.1,1.7,0.1),1)
  exact <- max(0,1-k/sqrt(3)); cheb <- 1/k^2
  vals <- round(c(exact,cheb,1-cheb,k/sqrt(3),1/k),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $X\sim U(-\sqrt3,\sqrt3)$, então $E(X)=0$ e $\operatorname{Var}(X)=1$. Para $k=`r k`$, qual é o valor exato de $P(|X|\geq k)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
O comprimento total do suporte é $2\sqrt3$, e o intervalo central $(-k,k)$ tem comprimento $2k$. Logo,
$$P(|X|\geq k)=1-\frac{k}{\sqrt3}
=`r sprintf("%.4f",exact)`.$$
Chebyshev daria apenas o limite superior $1/k^2=`r sprintf("%.4f",cheb)`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S10Q6_exato_vs_Chebyshev
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
